title: agrimonia eupatoria subvar. longifolia
intro: Agrimonia eupatoria subvar. longifolia, also known as Agrimony or Stickleback, is a perennial herb belonging to the rose family and is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which help to soothe skin irritations, reduce bleeding, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Agrimonia eupatoria subvar. longifolia include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers, as well as its potential to reduce symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed as a way to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
